skill.name: scientific-slides
skill.description: Create research presentation decks with scientific structure, visual design guidance, timing advice, Beamer templates, PDF assembly, validation scripts, and optional AI-generated slide images.
skill.summary: Create polished scientific slides for conferences, seminars, defenses, and research talks.
skill.author: K-Dense-AI

security_audit.summary: Most static blocker alerts are false positives caused by LaTeX commands, markdown examples, color hex values, and presentation terminology. No prompt injection or malicious intent was found, but the optional AI slide workflow sends prompts and attached images to OpenRouter and handles an API key. Publish with a clear privacy and credential-handling warning.

medium_findings.title: External AI API Receives Prompts and Attached Images
medium_findings.description: The slide generation workflow sends user prompts and optional attached images to the OpenRouter chat completions API. This is legitimate for AI slide generation, but users could accidentally transmit unpublished figures, private research data, or institutional logos to a third party.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The code clearly builds API requests to OpenRouter and base64-encodes attached local images into request content. The behavior is documented and user initiated, so it is a privacy risk rather than evidence of malicious intent.

medium_findings.title: API Key Passed Through Child Process Arguments
medium_findings.description: The wrapper script reads OPENROUTER_API_KEY and forwards it to the AI script with the --api-key command-line option. Command-line arguments can be exposed through process listings or logs on shared systems.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The command array explicitly appends the API key value before subprocess.run. This is a concrete credential-handling weakness, although the key is used for the intended API client.

medium_findings.title: Broad Environment File Discovery
medium_findings.description: The AI generation script searches the current directory, parent directories, and package parents for .env files. This may load credentials from a broader location than the user expects, although the script only looks for the OpenRouter key.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The search behavior is explicit and can affect local credential scope. No evidence shows arbitrary secret exfiltration beyond the OpenRouter credential used for API requests.

low_findings.title: Static Analyzer Blocker Alerts Are Contextual False Positives
low_findings.description: The reported C2, Windows SAM, weak crypto, and many backtick findings resolve to benign presentation content. Examples include charcoal color code #2C2C2C, business metrics TAM/SAM/SOM, markdown command examples, and LaTeX or design text rather than malicious code.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Targeted review confirmed the cited lines are design colors, business acronyms, or installation documentation. These contexts do not indicate command and control, Windows credential access, weak cryptography, or privilege abuse.

low_findings.title: External Commands Are Bounded but Require User Awareness
low_findings.description: The scripts use subprocess to invoke the bundled AI script and pdflatex with argument lists rather than shell strings. This reduces command injection risk, but validation of untrusted TeX files still runs a local compiler and should be treated as active content processing.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The subprocess calls are real and use user-selected files, but they avoid shell=True and include a timeout for LaTeX compilation. This supports a low residual execution risk rather than a high command injection finding.

dangerous_patterns.title: External AI API Receives Prompts and Attached Images
dangerous_patterns.description: The slide generation workflow sends user prompts and optional attached images to the OpenRouter chat completions API. This is legitimate for AI slide generation, but users could accidentally transmit unpublished figures, private research data, or institutional logos to a third party.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The code clearly builds API requests to OpenRouter and base64-encodes attached local images into request content. The behavior is documented and user initiated, so it is a privacy risk rather than evidence of malicious intent.

dangerous_patterns.title: API Key Passed Through Child Process Arguments
dangerous_patterns.description: The wrapper script reads OPENROUTER_API_KEY and forwards it to the AI script with the --api-key command-line option. Command-line arguments can be exposed through process listings or logs on shared systems.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The command array explicitly appends the API key value before subprocess.run. This is a concrete credential-handling weakness, although the key is used for the intended API client.

content.value_statement: Researchers need talks that explain complex work clearly without dense, text-heavy slides. This skill helps Claude, Codex, and Claude Code plan, design, generate, assemble, and validate scientific presentations.
actual_capabilities: Plans slide structure for conference talks, seminars, defenses, journal clubs, grant pitches, and tutorials.
Provides design guidance for scientific figures, visual hierarchy, timing, and audience-specific presentation flow.
Includes LaTeX Beamer templates for conference, seminar, and defense decks.
Generates complete slide images or slide visuals through OpenRouter when an API key is provided.
Attaches existing figures, charts, logos, or diagrams as context for AI slide generation.
Combines generated slide images into PDF files and validates PDF, PowerPoint, or Beamer presentations.
limitations: AI image generation requires an OpenRouter API key and sends prompts to an external service.
Attached figures and images may be transmitted to OpenRouter during AI generation.
PowerPoint assembly depends on a separate PPTX workflow rather than this skill alone.
Generated slides still need human review for scientific accuracy, citation quality, and institutional rules.

use_cases.title: Prepare a Conference Talk
use_cases.description: Turn a paper or result summary into a focused 10 to 20 minute conference deck with clear figures and timing.

use_cases.title: Build a Thesis Defense Deck
use_cases.description: Organize background, methods, results, limitations, and contributions into a polished defense presentation.

use_cases.title: Create a Technical Seminar
use_cases.description: Design a longer seminar that balances technical depth, visual explanation, and audience engagement.

prompt_templates.title: Plan a Basic Research Talk
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a slide-by-slide plan for a 15 minute conference talk about my research topic. Include title, purpose, key message, suggested visual, and timing for each slide.

prompt_templates.title: Improve an Existing Deck
prompt_templates.prompt: Review my current slide outline for a scientific audience. Identify weak narrative flow, overloaded slides, missing visuals, and timing problems. Suggest specific revisions.

prompt_templates.title: Generate Figure-Centered Slides
prompt_templates.prompt: Create prompts for AI-generated slide images that use my attached results figures. Keep a consistent visual style, include concise citations, and emphasize the main finding on each slide.

prompt_templates.title: Produce and Validate a Full Presentation
prompt_templates.prompt: Build a complete workflow for a research presentation from outline to generated slide images, PDF assembly, and final validation. Include privacy checks before using external AI generation.

output_examples.input: A 12 minute talk on a new machine learning method for microscopy images.
output: A 10 slide structure with title, motivation, method overview, dataset, results, ablation, limitations, and conclusion.
Suggested visuals for each slide, including model diagram, performance chart, and qualitative image examples.
Timing guidance that reserves the most time for method and results slides.

output_examples.input: A thesis defense deck with too many dense bullet slides.
output: A revised narrative arc that separates background, research questions, studies, contributions, and future work.
Recommendations to replace text-heavy slides with figure-led layouts and short verbal prompts.
A checklist for readability, consistency, timing, and backup slides.

output_examples.input: A set of existing charts that need to become presentation slides.
output: Slide prompts that attach each chart as context and ask for a clean title, key takeaway, and consistent design.
Warnings to remove sensitive data before sending attachments to an external AI service.
A PDF assembly plan using numbered slide images in presentation order.

best_practices: Start with the talk duration and audience before choosing slide count, depth, and visual style.
Use one main message per slide and let figures carry the scientific evidence.
Remove confidential data from prompts and attachments before using external AI slide generation.
anti_patterns: Creating dense bullet lists that force the audience to read instead of listen.
Changing colors, fonts, or visual style across slides in the same deck.
Uploading unpublished or sensitive figures to external AI services without permission.

faq.question: Does this skill create PowerPoint files directly?
faq.answer: It focuses on slide planning, visuals, Beamer templates, PDF assembly, and validation. PowerPoint assembly uses a separate PPTX workflow.

faq.question: Do I need an API key?
faq.answer: Only AI image generation requires an OpenRouter API key. Planning, guidance, templates, and local validation can be used without it.

faq.question: Can it use my existing figures?
faq.answer: Yes. The workflow supports attaching charts, diagrams, logos, and result figures as context for generated slide images.

faq.question: Is it safe for unpublished research?
faq.answer: Use caution. Prompts and attachments used for AI image generation are sent to OpenRouter, so remove confidential content first.

faq.question: What presentation formats are supported?
faq.answer: The skill supports image-based PDF decks, LaTeX Beamer templates, and guidance for PowerPoint workflows.

faq.question: Can it validate a finished presentation?
faq.answer: Yes. Included scripts check slide count, dimensions, file size, PowerPoint text issues, PDF properties, and Beamer compilation.
